title: knema korthalsii subsp. korthalsii
intro: Knema korthalsii subsp. korthalsii, also known as the Korthals' nut or Korthals' nut tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Myristicaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. The medicinal properties of Knema korthalsii subsp. korthalsii include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits. The tree's seeds, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and capsules, to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
